# Implementation Plan: Internal/Screen API OpenAPI Documentation

**Branch**: `001-frontend-api-docs` | **Date**: 2026-03-17 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `.specify/specs/001-frontend-api-docs/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

**Goal**: Auto-generate OpenAPI 3.x specifications for all internal/screen API endpoints in `src/OrganisationRegistry.Api/Backoffice` controllers, enabling interactive Swagger UI documentation for Angular SPA front-end developers.

**Technical Approach**: 
- Enhance existing C# Swashbuckle/Swagger infrastructure (already used for public APIs) to include internal API controllers
- Generate OpenAPI spec from code annotations (C# attributes) rather than manual maintenance
- Host Swagger UI using current approach (match public API documentation hosting)
- Ensure 100% endpoint coverage with interactive "Try It Out" testing capability

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: C# with .NET 6, nullable reference types enabled  
**Primary Dependencies**: Swashbuckle/Swagger (already integrated), Asp.Net Core, FluentValidation  
**Storage**: N/A — this is documentation generation, no data storage  
**Testing**: xUnit (already integrated), AggregateSource.Testing framework  
**Target Platform**: ASP.NET Core API (Linux server deployment)  
**Project Type**: API documentation generation (documenting existing REST API endpoints)  
**Performance Goals**: OpenAPI spec generation on each build (no runtime performance impact)  
**Constraints**: 
- Must not modify API behavior or add new endpoints (documentation only)
- Must match existing OpenAPI version used for public API docs
- Swagger UI must support interactive testing without code generation
- All internal API controllers in `src/OrganisationRegistry.Api/Backoffice` MUST be included
**Scale/Scope**: 126 internal API controllers in Backoffice, 500+ endpoints to document (actual count per codebase audit)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### I. Event Sourcing is Immutable Law
**Status**: ✅ COMPLIANT
- This feature documents existing APIs; no events are created or modified
- Documentation generation does not touch event store or aggregates

### II. Commands and Events Drive All State Changes
**Status**: ✅ COMPLIANT
- Documentation only; no new commands or events introduced
- Existing command/event patterns remain unchanged

### III. CQRS: Commands and Queries are Architecturally Separate
**Status**: ✅ COMPLIANT
- Documentation reflects existing CQRS architecture (queries to Backoffice controllers, which map to domain)
- No architectural changes required

### IV. Respect Aggregate Boundaries
**Status**: ✅ COMPLIANT
- Documentation exposes API contracts (what clients see), not internal aggregate state
- Existing aggregate boundaries remain unchanged

### V. Testing is Not Optional
**Status**: ✅ COMPLIANT
- Documentation generation is testable: Swagger UI can be validated for endpoint coverage
- Existing unit and integration tests remain unchanged
- New requirement: validate OpenAPI spec matches actual API behavior (auto-generated reduces drift)

### Conclusion
✅ **Gate passes**: Feature respects all core principles. Documentation generation is non-invasive and aligns with existing architecture.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
.specify/specs/001-frontend-api-docs/
├── plan.md              # This file (implementation plan)
├── spec.md              # Feature specification
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (research findings)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (API data model)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (OpenAPI contract definitions)
│   └── openapi-spec.yaml # Generated OpenAPI 3.x specification
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (developer quickstart)
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md   # Quality checklist (pre-planning)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command)
```

### Source Code (repository)

```text
src/OrganisationRegistry.Api/
├── Backoffice/          # Internal/screen API controllers (PRIMARY SCOPE)
│   ├── Organisation/
│   ├── Delegations/
│   ├── Contact/
│   ├── Building/
│   └── [...other entities]
├── Search/              # Public search API (secondary: already documented)
├── [Other controllers]
└── Security/            # Authorization attributes (referenced in docs)

# Build & Documentation Artifacts
docs/
├── api/
│   ├── swagger-internal.yaml    # Generated from Backoffice controllers
│   ├── swagger-public.yaml      # Existing public API spec
│   └── swagger-ui/              # Hosting directory (if separate from API)

# Build configuration (existing, to be enhanced)
.sln files               # Visual Studio solution with Swashbuckle NuGet packages
paket.dependencies      # Central package version management
```

**Structure Decision**: 
- Feature documents existing internal API controllers in `src/OrganisationRegistry.Api/Backoffice/`
- Leverages existing Swashbuckle infrastructure already configured for public APIs
- Generates OpenAPI spec as build artifact
- Hosts Swagger UI using current approach (determined during implementation phase)

## Phase 0: Research & Unknowns

**Status**: No critical unknowns. All technical decisions clarified during `/speckit.clarify`:
- ✅ OpenAPI version: Match existing public API version
- ✅ Generation method: Auto-generate from C# Swashbuckle annotations
- ✅ Scope: All internal/screen APIs in Backoffice controllers
- ✅ Hosting: Match current approach for public API docs

**Research Output**: `research.md` (to be generated)
